q: To study the structural integrity of metallic components and parts in some applications, which type of internal study technique is used?
o: To find out whether the metal is good or bad
To detect cracks inside the metal
To check the density of the metal
To detect the polishing of the metal

q: Which of the following has a variable composition?
If the speed of a moving object is doubled, then keeping its mass constant, how many times will its momentum become compared to that at the initial speed?
Which of the following is NOT a cause of refraction (deviation) of light?
o: Change in speed of light due to change in medium
Change in wavelength of light due to change in medium

q: Regarding baking soda (sodium hydrogen carbonate), which of the following statements is NOT correct?
A car runs at a speed of 62 km/hr for the first 2.5 hours and then at a speed of 48 km/hr for 1.5 hours. Its average speed is approximately ______.
Which of the following statements is correct?
o: Magnetic field lines start from the south pole of a bar magnet and end at its north pole
Magnetic field lines never intersect each other
Magnetic field lines are denser where the magnetic field is stronger
Magnetic field lines are straight near the poles of a bar magnet

q: By which process does water move from the soil into the roots of plants?
Which of the following human female body parts prepares itself every month to receive a fertilised egg?
Which primary toxic substance allows mitochondria (the cell's powerhouses) to function?
What is the name of the semiconservative process in which DNA duplicates into two identical molecules?
In plants, in which part of the body is the dividing tissue region located, which helps in growth?
Which of the following statements is NOT correct?
o: Gravitational force acts effectively between very small particles
The value of g changes from place to place
The greater the mass of an object, the more strongly gravity attracts it
Gravitational force has an infinite range

q: If the kinetic energy of a moving object is 450 J and its speed is 15 m/s, then what will be the mass of the object?
When parallel rays of light coming from a distant object fall on a concave mirror, after reflection at which point will these rays be focused?
How many oxygen atoms are present in H2O?
When a straight wire carrying current passes through the centre of each loop of a ______, it produces a magnetic field.
An ozone molecule is made up of ______.
What happens when an insect sucks the juice from the leaves of a crop plant?
o: The growth of the plant is affected
The plant is killed by being eaten away

q: When concentrated cold H2SO4 reacts with phenol in the presence of phenol, then ______ is produced.
Which of the following is an organic compound?
By how much is the body temperature of a snake lower than the temperature of its surroundings?
Which of the following is used as a standard reference for defining relative atomic mass?
Which of the following is a correct use of baking soda (sodium hydrogen carbonate) in daily life?
o: It is used to prepare edible salts
It is used to make soaps non-greasy
It is used in fire extinguishers to produce foam
It is used to neutralise strong acids in lead-acid batteries

q: In fish production, which of the following comes under the category of shellfish?
Find the arithmetic mean of the numbers 96, 70, 85, 52, 40, 88, 63, 40 and 60.
1/20 is what percentage of 11/50? (Round off the answer to two decimal places.)
A typist takes 40 minutes to type 2 pages. How much time will he take to type 101 pages? (in hours)
A shopkeeper keeps the marked price of his goods 75% above the cost price and gives a discount of 52% on it. Find the loss or profit percentage in this transaction.
The average of 10 players of a team is 57 runs. If the 11th player makes 195 runs, then by how much will their average increase?
Simplify (5x + 11y)^2 + (5x - 11y)^2.

The difference between the present ages of two brothers is 4 years. Twenty years ago, the elder brother's age was 1.25 times the younger brother's age. What is the present age of the younger brother (in years)?
A person is standing at a distance of 36 metres from the base of a tower. The angle of elevation of the top of the tower is θ, where tanθ = 5/3. Find the height of the tower (in metres).

In an election between two candidates, 80% of the voters cast their votes, and out of these, 2% votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 8,820 votes which was 75% of the total valid votes. Find the total number of voters on the voter list.

11 men and 17 women can complete a piece of work in 6 days, while 18 men and 15 women can complete the same work in 4 days. In how many days will the work done by 1 woman be equal to the work done by how many men?
A and B together can do a piece of work in 6 days. A alone can do the same work in 7 days. In how many days can B alone do double that work? (in days)
What is the LCM of 18, 4 and 8a?
The sides of a triangle are 39 cm, 62 cm and 85 cm. If the side of length 62 cm is taken as the base, find its height.
A shopkeeper sells an item at a profit of 32%. If he had bought it for 57% less and sold it for the same price, find the new profit percentage.
